sessionSummary: Phase 2 PR-3 validation keeps the importance-tier resolver authoritative while the managed-frontmatter rewrite re-emits both serialized tier locations from the same resolved value. This fixture models the historical drift case where an explicit important-tier save could later be normalized back to a default-looking frontmatter tier if migration treated the serializer as a second authority.

filesModified.description: Managed-frontmatter rewrite now synchronizes frontmatter and MEMORY METADATA importance_tier from a single resolved value

keyDecisions.decision: Use session-extractor as the canonical importance-tier resolver
keyDecisions.rationale: PR-3 treats migration and post-save review as consumers of the resolved tier rather than separate authorities

nextSteps: Run the targeted PR-3 vitest coverage
Replay the fixture through generate-context.js and confirm tier parity
